inference_steps: [183s to 186s] The earlier 183s-187s window remains a rejected candidate. Chronologically, frames 183s-186s show ribbon-cutting rather than a slide scene, so they do not ground the queried event.

[187s] Frame 187s is also not the answer-critical event: it shows a child with a blue-haired, blue-crowned, blue-and-white-dressed character on the ground, with only a slide-like structure in the background, and that character is explicitly described as not matching Donald Duck, Daisy, Goofy, Mickey Mouse, or Pluto.

[62s to 64s; 215s to 218s; 273s to 275s] A separate visual-temporal grounding pass identifies three candidate intervals for the queried event: 62s-64s, 215s-218s, and 273s-275s. These are the relevant windows to inspect instead of the rejected 183s-187s sequence.

[62s to 64s; 215s to 217s] Frames were then retrieved from the grounded event windows, including 62s, 63s, 64s, 215s, 216s, and 217s, providing local visual evidence for the slide-accompaniment event.

[62s to 64s] In the newly grounded 62s-64s local sequence, the visual evidence shows a large brown dog character at the bottom of a slide structure with children present. This is the only new evidence that directly ties a listed character to the slide scene.

[62s to 64s; 187s] That dog character is identified as Pluto, which maps uniquely to option E among the choices. The rejected 187s candidate does not match any option, whereas the grounded slide-scene character does.

evidence.evidence_text: Frames were returned as a chronological neighboring sequence around the requested timestamp or temporal anchor at 183s, 184s, 185s, 186s, and 187s.

In frames 183-186, the scene is ribbon-cutting and no slide is visible. In frame 187, the only character-child interaction shows a blue-haired, blue-crowned, blue-and-white-dressed character holding hands with a toddler girl on the ground in a playground setting; a red-and-white slide-like structure is only in the background, and the visible character is described as not matching Donald Duck, Daisy, Goofy, Mickey Mouse, or Pluto.
The queried event appears at three candidate intervals: 62.00s-64.00s, 215.00s-218.00s, and 273.00s-275.00s.
Retrieved candidate frames from the grounded event windows at 62s, 63s, 64s, 215s, 216s, and 217s.

In the newly retrieved event-centered frames, a large brown dog character is shown at the bottom of a slide structure with children present, and the dog character is identified as Pluto, matching option E.
